subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Snake Venom Science
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Snake Venom Science.

hook.text: Snake venom is a cocktail of 50-100 different toxins, each targeting a different body system.

buildup.text: Hemotoxins destroy blood cells and prevent clotting. Neurotoxins paralyze muscles.

discovery.text: Cytotoxins destroy tissue around the bite, causing massive local damage.

twist.text: Antivenom is made by injecting tiny amounts of venom into horses and harvesting antibodies.

climax.text: Most snakebite deaths occur in rural areas far from hospitals with antivenom supplies.

punchline.text: Snake venom is a precision weapon. Antivenom is the shield—if you can reach it.

quiz.question: How is antivenom typically produced?
options: By injecting venom into horses and harvesting antibodies
By synthesizing it in a lab
By extracting it from immune snakes
